description: The Zemun Clan is a Serbian mafia organization based in Zemun, a municipality of Belgrade. It rose to prominence between 2000 and 2003, becoming one of the most powerful criminal groups in Southeast Europe. The clan is involved in drug trafficking, arms smuggling, extortion, and contract killings. Its leaders included Dušan Spasojević and Milorad Ulemek, and it had ties to Serbian state security services. The group was heavily targeted after the assassination of Prime Minister Zoran Đinđić in 2003.

country_links.evidence_quote: En noviembre de 2009, la Policía argentina detuvo a cinco narcotraficantes serbios y se apoderaron de sus 492 kilos de cocaína en Buenos Aires.
country_links.context: The Zemun Clan's drugs were seized in Argentina, indicating its trafficking network operated there.

country_links.evidence_quote: Las ruta de las drogas era de Uruguay y Argentina a Sudáfrica hacia el norte de Italia , Turquía y Montenegro .
country_links.context: The Zemun Clan's drug route ended in northern Italy.

country_links.evidence_quote: Las ruta de las drogas era de Uruguay y Argentina a Sudáfrica hacia el norte de Italia , Turquía y Montenegro .
country_links.context: The Zemun Clan's drug route included Montenegro.

country_links.evidence_quote: Un paquete de mensajería de cinco kilos de cocaína fue interceptado desde Paraguay y cuatro belgradenses fueron arrestados.
country_links.context: The Zemun Clan intercepted a cocaine package from Paraguay, indicating a supply link.
